name: Base-image VIPM tooling
description: THIS REPO's VIPM packages baked into the CI worker image - preserved across tooling updates (it is in the catalog's userConfig.files), so your additions are never clobbered. build-tooling-vipc.py merges these OVER the tooling-pushed defaults in ci-tooling.defaults.json (your entries win on a name collision; a differing default version is surfaced as a notice) and turns the result into ci-tooling.vipc, which the Dockerfile's VIPM hook applies at image-build time (vipm apply_vipc). Edit this list + regenerate (Reconfigure) to change your repo's baked-in tooling; new tooling defaults arrive additively via ci-tooling.defaults.json when you take a new version.

target.name: LabVIEW
target.comment: labviewVersion must match the LabVIEW in the NI base image. The generator maps it to the .vipc Target <Version> (2026 -> '26.1 (64-bit)'); install-vipc.ps1 passes the same year to `vipm apply_vipc -labview_version`.

vipmPackages.comment: Top-level packages only - the CORE dependency set every CI action runner needs. Each is emitted as a <Package Included=false> entry, so `vipm apply_vipc` downloads it (and its dependencies, listed here for reference only) from the configured VIPM repository at build time. Pin `version` for reproducible images; set it to null to resolve to the latest available. `include:false` documents a package without baking it in. Capability-specific dependencies (e.g. Antidoc) do NOT live here - they live in their own opt-in VIPC under .github/labview/<capability>/<capability>.vipc and are baked into the worker image only when that capability is installed (see alreadyBakedElsewhere).

packages.name: jki_lib_caraya
packages.summary: Caraya Unit Test Framework by JKI - assertion-based unit testing; 'your VI is your test'.

packages.name: jki_vi_tester
packages.summary: JKI VI Tester - the classic xUnit-style LabVIEW unit test framework.
packages.note: Package name taken from the JKI VI Tester toolkit namespace (__jki_vi_tester). Version left null (latest) because it could not be pinned from a verified source; pin it here once confirmed in VIPM.

packages.name: ni_lib_utf_junit_report
packages.summary: UTF JUnit Report by NI - generates a JUnit-compatible report file from LabVIEW Unit Test Framework (UTF) results; required by the built-in 'LabVIEWCLI -OperationName RunUnitTests' operation.
packages.note: REQUIRED for headless UTF: the RunUnitTests CLI operation ships in the container but its VI tree links against this library's JUnit-report VIs. Without it the operation is broken and fails with LabVIEWCLI error -350053 ('missing or bad files ... in the operation folder'). NI-published on VIPM (Apache 2.0, LabVIEW>=13.0, Win/Mac/Linux); 1.0.1.43 is the only release. Requires the UTF toolkit (ni-utf-labview-support), which the Dockerfile installs via nipkg.

packages.name: ni_lib_junit_results_api
packages.summary: JUnit Results API by NI - LabVIEW API for building and writing JUnit-format test result files; the results-model library underneath the UTF JUnit report path.
packages.note: Added alongside ni_lib_utf_junit_report so the headless 'LabVIEWCLI -OperationName RunUnitTests' JUnit output has its full VI tree baked into the worker image.

packages.name: dataflow_g_lib_g_image
packages.summary: G Image by dataflow_g - read/write/encode images (PNG/JPEG/BMP) in LabVIEW; used by the VI Browser snapshot generator to embed the rendered diagram and front-panel images in its HTML output.
packages.note: Action-runner tooling, not a project dependency: the VI Snapshots (VI Browser 1.0) HTML generator uses it to encode/embed images, so it lives in the core CI tooling rather than in any one project's VIPC.

alreadyBakedElsewhere.comment: Listed for completeness so this manifest is a full picture of the worker's tooling. These are NOT emitted into ci-tooling.vipc to avoid double-installing.

items.what: VI Analyzer full default test set
items.via: labview-ci.Dockerfile installs it directly (nipkg); not a VIPM package.

items.package: (discovered from the NI feed by name match; override with the UTF_PACKAGE build-arg)
items.via: labview-ci.Dockerfile installs it directly (nipkg), mirroring the VI Analyzer support package. UTF is an NI product (not on VIPM), so it comes from the NI Package Manager feed, not ci-tooling.vipc. run-unit-tests.ps1 runs the tests headlessly with the built-in 'LabVIEWCLI -OperationName RunUnitTests' operation that ships with the LabVIEW command line interface in the container. NOTE: that operation also needs the 'ni_lib_utf_junit_report' VIPM package (see vipmPackages above) to emit its JUnit report - without it RunUnitTests fails with -350053.

items.package: oglib_file, oglib_array, oglib_error, oglib_lvdata, oglib_string, oglib_appcontrol
items.via: Project-owned dependencies live in the project's own VIPC (example/Dependencies.vipc), staged + applied by the image build; OpenG is also pulled automatically as a Caraya dependency. (G Image / dataflow_g_lib_g_image now lives in ci-tooling above because the snapshot generator uses it.)

Capability-specific - lives in .github/labview/antidoc/antidoc.vipc (its own opt-in VIPC), NOT in ci-tooling.vipc. The image build stages and bakes it ONLY when the Antidoc capability is installed (its run-antidoc workflow is present), so repos that do not generate docs never carry the dependency.

todo.comment: Items the user requested that need a non-VIPM install path. Tracked here rather than silently dropped.
